data.body:
DSO vs rebar PLAN-REVIEW GAP — full remediation evaluation session (2026-07)

INPUT: ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md (gap analysis of DSO's digital-service-orchestra planning review vs rebar's plan-review gate). GOAL: evaluate each remediation for DIRECTIONAL ALIGNMENT with rebar's architecture — many "solve problems rebar's architecture solves better at a different level." Evaluated one-at-a-time with the user; grounded in docs/plan-review-gate.md + criteria_routing.json.

GROUNDING LENS: rebar = four-pass find(P1)->verify(P2)->decide(P3 deterministic arithmetic: priority=validity x impact)->coach(P4 locked MOVE_REGISTRY). DET floor (P1-P8) is the only default-blocking tier. REVIEW_RESULT sidecar records per-finding fingerprints. Principle applied throughout: adopt DSO's CONTENT (lexicons, named FP classes, calibration idioms), never its weaker MECHANISMS (self-scored persona committees, hard-gate anti-rationalization prose, lexical word-match). Keep the generic passes (P2 contract, P4 coach) generic across findings — criterion-specific logic lives in rubrics/evidence, not shared passes.

=== KEY GROUNDING FINDINGS (from code) ===
- F1 (ac-text-quality) is BLOCKING (default_posture=blocking, block_threshold=0.7) and its checklist ALREADY has in_session_evaluable: "Evaluable in-session via repo artifacts / closing PR CI / deterministic command — not post-sprint-only." So in-session testability is already a blocking property.
- E6 already has no_hedges (LLM-tier hedge detection) + cutover_exercised_e2e; E5 has no_proxy_validation — but E5/E6 are ADVISORY (0.95). => bypass-the-real-path incomplete implementations can still slip both gates (F1 catches only the coarse post-sprint case). Noted as a SEPARATE threshold-calibration track.
- G3.coverage_map already does the 4-bucket audit (fully/partial/uncovered/structural) => the DSO "scope-gap" pattern (excluded-by-all-children/owned-by-none) is ALREADY covered.
- G4.interaction_modes already includes a consumer-impact mode => G-5/G-10/G4 consumer-enumeration is a reliability upgrade (recall->worklist), not a new mode.
- Sidecar CANNOT currently measure bin-packing contamination: it stamps each finding's criteria but throws away chunk co-residency (only a chunk COUNT survives, coverage["chunks"]). Minimal fix: stamp each finding with its chunk cohort (sorted criterion-id set) in pass1_chunk (passes.py:294-306), carry through _slim (sidecar.py:236-264).

=== VERDICTS (every item) ===
ADOPTED (grouped into 10 workstreams under epic cite-stone-sea / 9f78-ccbf-9248-4097):
- WS1 Pass-2 vocabulary expansion: NEW generic sub-answers committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim (unifies G-4 exclusions + G-7a confident-assertions + G-7b hedges — keys on "a committed element (AC/task/edit/exclusion) depends on the claim AND no verification-OR-fallback"; materiality via "committed element", not subjective "load-bearing") and respects_artifact_altitude (FP-1); gloss evidence_entails_finding for restatement/null-delta (FP-2). na-defaulting.
- WS2 E4 detectors + exec:DET hedge detector: G-7a confident-assertion scan-protocol lexicon; G-4 exclusion sub-check at NORMAL priority (user: elevating to highest = overreach); G-7b DET hedge detector deduped vs E6.no_hedges, verifier judges SUBSTANCE not word-presence.
- WS3 Enumeration-overlay pattern: G1/G2 as code-grounded ROUTER emitting an overlay trigger; a DEDICATED overlay enumerates the invisible affected set in its OWN context window. Instantiated for G-5 (new-prohibition -> outlawed call sites) and G-10 (new ref/event/schedule -> workflow trigger filters + release-infra). This design (user's) is more reliable than the report's lexical trigger.
- WS4 DET-floor verify-command blacklist (G-3a): mechanically-checkable file-inspection/unanchored-grep/unquoted-metachar defects; polyglot fail-open abstain.
- WS5 Rubric enrichments (prompt-only): E6 verify-cmd defect taxonomy + corrected-command anchors; T3 (G-8) three empiricism axes + four-class evidence scale — T3-LOCAL (Pass-1 evidence[] + sidecar), ZERO Pass-2 change (generic likelihood grader reads richer evidence), spike stays Pass-4; G3 (G-11) three-part coverage standard + bypass/narrowing/partial patterns; T5c/T10 (FP-3a) ambiguity-not-absence access framing; T11 post-rollback SCHEMA-STATE assertion + DATA LOSS RISK; E2 defaultable-gaps coaching; G4 consumer-enumeration precursor.
- WS6 Reviewer self-calibration anchors: G-9 small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning + two-directional bar (A1/T5a); FP-3b explicit one-way blast_radius ratchet on the verifier.
- WS7 Prompt-hardening pass (eval-guarded): R-6 affirmative-framing/pink-elephant (organizing principle) + G-12 shared-scaffolding prompt-injection directive + FP-3c uniform forward-looking rule.
- WS8 Coach moves: ADD foundation/enhancement split (fidelity axis, distinct from move 7 scope axis); SHARPEN move 9 for verifiable-proxy. DROP DEFERRED_MEASUREMENT as a move — it is counter-architectural (would coach ACs that F1.in_session_evaluable BLOCKS and the fail-closed completion verifier cannot certify); post-hoc validation instead becomes a discovered_from follow-up ticket, keeping the current ticket in-session-closable.
- WS9 Sidecar cohort field (R-1 part 1): the observability prerequisite; unblocks fort-wisp-wren.
- WS10 Docs + authoring (R-3, R-5): R-3 document the stage-by-stage asymmetric-error invariant (so tuning cannot align two skepticisms); R-5 registry-derived longer-form Markdown authoring guide (section-keyed, CI-parity-gated) + anchored deep-links in coach output + --help epilog URL + rebar explain <criterion-id> across CLI/MCP/library + anchored-refusal idiom. Sequenced AFTER criteria work.

ROUTED TO DEDICATED DESIGN/PLACEHOLDER EPICS:
- G-1 conflict detection + G-2 oscillation (folded together) -> grumpy-gland-rose (1464-bd32-07a3-4225). Design tension: deterministic-over-coach-(move,subject)-tuples vs the report's new LLM pass.
- G-6 cross-ticket interaction -> only-crave-art (bd83-c56f-1f72-4ef2), enriched with DSO four-tier severity + named-resource/sub-file-region FP discipline + staged DET->LLM build. Confirmed: belongs OUTSIDE the gate (a per-ticket reviewer can't see the store).
- R-1 part 2 config-flagged per-criterion context isolation -> fort-wisp-wren (ca57-1627-85ed-484c). Evidence-gated on WS9's cohort data.
- R-4 -> spin-cub-usher (65dd-e93b-666a-4caa): reframed from "cite bugs in rubrics" to a project-adaptive calibration-example library (core + project overlay, learns over time). Open Qs: injection point(s), bounded-growth curation.

REJECTED (with rationale):
- R-2 double-verify blocking findings: misreads DSO — its committee = perspective DIVERSITY (distinct concern areas), which rebar already gets from distinct CRITERIA; re-running the same narrow typed question is resampling, cost for minimal value. Revisit only if verifier flakiness proves chronic.
- T9 RETIRE-row-always-filled: nit — "never retired" is the sensible default for a blank value.
- Section 7 items (persona committees / hard-gate prose / >=80% word-match): rejections confirmed — rebar's arithmetic-decide, code-enforced gates, and ISF semantic match are each strictly better.

NOTED, NO ACTION (separate track): E5.no_proxy_validation / E6.cutover_exercised_e2e are advisory => bypass-the-real-path incomplete implementations can slip both gates. Whether to elevate toward blocking / share one testability bar across both gates = threshold-calibration, handled outside this epic.

TICKET STRUCTURE CREATED: epic cite-stone-sea + WS1..WS10 stories (win-attic-wren, orb-jet-folk, catty-noun-lyre, male-welt-inlet, glad-gloom-bog, neat-spa-ulcer, snappy-yew-strafe, sip-thorn-epoch, dork-quota-zeal, rife-feint-canal), 4 relates_to links to the design/placeholder epics.
